title: dactylis glomerata subsp. reichenbachii
intro: Dactylis glomerata subsp. reichenbachii, commonly known as mountain grass or Reichenbach's cock's-foot, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Dactylis glomerata. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dactylis glomerata subsp. reichenbachii are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as improved digestion and reduced risk of cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
